title: John Williams
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
{{Otheruses|John|more=Williams}}
{{Cleanup}}
{{Person
|image=[[File:John_Williams.jpg]]
|name=John Williams
|birth=[[February 8]], [[1930s|1932]]<ref name="This Week! Ep2">{{ThisWeek|_xt_rQZaZSo|Battlefront II Updates, A Look Into Poe Dameron's Past, and More!|sw_url=video/battlefront-ii-updates-a-look-into-poe-dameron-s-past-and-more}}</ref>
|death=
|listedpronouns=<!--Only include pronouns if they have been explicitly specified by the individual to which this article pertains.-->
|nationality=American
|occupation=
|companies=
|website=
}}
{{Quote|Once again, John [Williams] has exceeded my expectations and produced a lavish, rich, moving and thrilling score. Every fan of Star Wars—and of great music—is in his debt.|[[George Lucas]] about John Williams' score for ''[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|The Phantom Menace]]''|<ref name="Sdtk">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ultimateeditionsoundtrack.com/message.htm|text=The Ultimate Edition -- Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|archivedate=20010713115622}}</ref>}}
'''John Towner Williams''' [[Wikipedia:Order of the British Empire|KBE]] (born [[February 8]], [[1930s#1932|1932]])<ref name="This Week! Ep2" /> is an American composer, conductor, and pianist. He has composed nearly all of the [[Musical score|music]] heard in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' films. He has also composed scores for multiple other well-known movies and television shows, including ''Indiana Jones'', ''Close Encounters of the Third Kind'' and ''Superman.'' Williams announced his intention to retire from film score composing after the release of [[Wikipedia:Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny|''Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny'']] in 2023 to focus more on composing independent orchestral and symphonic pieces, though he later rescinded this.

In [[September]] [[2022]], John Williams and former [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] chief [[Bob Iger (chief executive officer)|Bob Iger]] were each made an honorary [[Wikipedia:Order of the British Empire|Knight of the Order of the British Empire]] by [[Wikipedia:Monarchy of the United Kingdom|Queen]] [[Wikipedia:Elizabeth II|Elizabeth II]], with Williams being approved for his services to film music as a composer and conductor in the [[Wikipedia:United Kingdom|United Kingdom]].<ref name="KBE">{{WebCite|url=https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/honorary-british-awards-to-foreign-nationals-2022/honorary-awards-to-foreign-nationals-in-2022|text=Honorary awards to foreign nationals in 2022|author=[[Wikipedia:Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office|Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office]]|work=[[Wikipedia:gov.uk|GOV.UK]]|archivedate=20220924223832}}</ref>

==Biography==
[[File:Maestro_Williams.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Williams conducting the [[London Symphony Orchestra]]]]
In 1977, he composed the music for ''[[Star Wars]]'', which turned out to be a huge success. ''Star Wars'' became the best-selling score-only soundtrack of all time and won him an Oscar.

Williams's son, [[Joseph Williams]], chose a musical career like his father. The two worked together on [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']].

[[Rick McCallum]] stated at [[Star Wars Reunion II]] that he had hoped to have John Williams on the musical score for the [[Star Wars: Underworld|Star Wars live-action TV series]] and added that each episode would have had its own original score.

Williams announced his intention to retire from film score composing after the release of [[Wikipedia:Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny|''Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny'']] in 2023 to focus more on composing independent orchestral and symphonic pieces, though he later rescinded this.<ref name="comicbook.com">{{WebCite|url=https://comicbook.com/movies/news/john-williams-walks-back-retirement-claims/?fbclid=IwAR1nCy06yy3sFcYx5HReglXZ6ZnZ7ksyCZ5v0NqtDJOBBivIbFQqF6GTM04&mibextid=Zxz2cZ|text=John Williams Walks Back Retirement Claims|archiveurl=https://archive.is/kJcEh}}</ref>
